docs.title: azure-functions-knowledge-python
docs.text: Knowledge retrieval (RAG) decorators for Azure Functions Python v2.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Decorator-based API — Seamless integration with Azure Functions Python v2 programming model
  • Provider abstraction — Pluggable knowledge providers via protocol-based interface
  • Notion support — Built-in Notion provider for searching and retrieving pages
  • Async support — Automatic async offloading for non-blocking execution
  • Environment variable resolution — %VAR% placeholder substitution for secure credential handling

docs.title: Change Workflow
docs.text:
1. Confirm whether the change affects knowledge API, provider behavior, decorator interface, or docs only.
  2. Update examples when they are used as public contract material.
  3. Keep tests and docs in lockstep for public behavior changes.
  4. Do not broaden supported Python versions or dependency ranges casually.

docs.text:
• make test
  • make lint
  • make typecheck
  • make security
  • make build

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
1. Install the package with the Notion provider:
pip install azure-functions-knowledge-python[notion]
  1. Set up your Notion API token as an environment variable:
export NOTION_TOKEN="your-notion-integration-token"

docs.title: Data Injection with input
docs.text:
The input decorator searches a knowledge provider and injects results directly into your handler:

import azure.functions as func
from azure_functions_knowledge import Document, KnowledgeBindings

app = func.FunctionApp()
kb = KnowledgeBindings()

@app.route(route="search", methods=["GET"])
@kb.input(
    "docs",
    provider="notion",
    query=lambda req: req.params.get("q", ""),
    top=5,
    connection="%NOTION_TOKEN%",
)
def search(req: func.HttpRequest, docs: list[Document]) -> func.HttpResponse:
    import json
    results = [{"title": d.title, "url": d.url} for d in docs]
    return func.HttpResponse(json.dumps(results), mimetype="application/json")

docs.title: Static vs Dynamic Queries
docs.text:
Static query — Same search every invocation:

@kb.input("docs", provider="notion", query="project roadmap", connection="%NOTION_TOKEN%")
def handler(timer, docs: list[Document]) -> None:
    ...

Dynamic query — Derived from handler parameters:

@kb.input(
    "docs",
    provider="notion",
    query=lambda req: req.params.get("q", ""),
    connection="%NOTION_TOKEN%",
)
def handler(req: func.HttpRequest, docs: list[Document]) -> func.HttpResponse:
    ...

docs.title: Client Injection with inject_client
docs.text:
For imperative control, use inject_client to receive a provider instance:

@app.route(route="page/{page_id}", methods=["GET"])
@kb.inject_client("client", provider="notion", connection="%NOTION_TOKEN%")
def get_page(req: func.HttpRequest, client) -> func.HttpResponse:
    import json
    page_id = req.route_params.get("page_id", "")
    doc = client.get_document(page_id)
    return func.HttpResponse(
        json.dumps({"title": doc.title, "content": doc.content}),
        mimetype="application/json",
    )

docs.title: Decorator Composition Rules
docs.text:
• Azure decorators go outermost, knowledge decorators closest to the function
  • input and inject_client are mutually exclusive on the same handler
  • No decorator can be applied twice to the same handler

docs.title: Connection Strings
docs.text:
Connection parameters support %VAR% environment variable substitution:

connection="%NOTION_TOKEN%"          # Single env var
connection="Bearer %API_KEY%"        # Partial substitution
connection={"token": "%MY_TOKEN%"}   # Mapping with substitution

docs.title: Async Support
docs.text:
Both input and inject_client automatically detect async handlers and offload blocking I/O via asyncio.to_thread():

@app.route(route="search", methods=["GET"])
@kb.input("docs", provider="notion", query="roadmap", connection="%NOTION_TOKEN%")
async def search(req: func.HttpRequest, docs: list[Document]) -> func.HttpResponse:
    ...

docs.title: Basic Installation
docs.text: pip install azure-functions-knowledge-python

docs.title: With Notion Provider
docs.text: pip install azure-functions-knowledge-python[notion]

docs.title: With All Providers
docs.text: pip install azure-functions-knowledge-python[all]

docs.title: Development Installation
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/yeongseon/azure-functions-knowledge-python.git
cd azure-functions-knowledge-python
make install

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.11+
  • Azure Functions Python v2 programming model

docs.title: Release Process
docs.text: This document outlines the steps to release a new version of azure-functions-knowledge-python to PyPI and update the changelog using the existing Makefile and Hatch-based workflows.

docs.title: Step 1: Bump Version and Generate Changelog
docs.text:
Use Makefile targets to bump the version and update the changelog:

make release-patch     # Patch release (e.g., v0.10.0 -> v0.10.1)
make release-minor     # Minor release (e.g., v0.10.1 -> v0.11.0)
make release-major     # Major release (e.g., v0.11.0 -> v1.0.0)

Each command will:

  1. Update the version in src/azure_functions_knowledge/__init__.py
  2. Generate or update CHANGELOG.md via git-cliff
  3. Commit the version bump and changelog
  4. Create a Git tag (e.g., v0.11.0) and push to main

Make sure your main branch is up-to-date before running these commands.

docs.title: Changelog Generation
docs.text: The changelog is generated automatically by git-cliff from conventional commit messages.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
• cliff.toml - defines commit grouping, categories, and output format
  • Makefile - make changelog runs git-cliff -o CHANGELOG.md

docs.title: Commit Message Convention
docs.text:
Follow Conventional Commits for proper changelog grouping:

Prefix Changelog Category feat: Features fix: Bug Fixes docs: Documentation refactor: Refactor style: Styling test: Testing perf: Performance ci: / chore: Miscellaneous Tasks build: Other

Use scopes for more context: fix(openapi): preserve explicit 200 response

docs.title: Manual Changelog Regeneration
docs.text:
make changelog           # Regenerate CHANGELOG.md from all tags
make commit-changelog    # Stage and commit the updated changelog

docs.title: Step 2: Build and Test the Package
docs.text:
make build

To test the local build:

pip install dist/azure_functions_knowledge-<version>-py3-none-any.whl

docs.title: Step 3: Publish to PyPI
docs.text:
make publish-pypi
  • Uses hatch publish under the hood
  • Relies on ~/.pypirc for authentication (must contain PyPI token)

docs.title: Step 4: (Optional) Publish to TestPyPI
docs.text:
make publish-test

To install from TestPyPI:

pip install --index-url https://test.pypi.org/simple/ azure-functions-knowledge-python

docs.title: Summary of Makefile Commands
docs.text: Task Command Version bump + changelog make release-patch / release-minor / release-major Build distributions make build Publish to PyPI make publish-pypi Publish to TestPyPI make publish-test Regenerate changelog only make changelog Show current version make version
